//: Shared link-rewrite fixture cases, run by both .github/scripts/tests/test_publish_wiki_link_rewrite.py (against publish_wiki.py's transform_links) and frontend/scripts/generate-docs-site.test.js (against generate-docs-site.js's transformLinks). sourcePath is relative to ./fixture_repo, resolved against ./map.json (a small, fixed, standalone mapping — not the real wiki-publish-map.json). Each case gives either a single `expected` output (both implementations must produce it identically) or a `pythonExpected`/`jsExpected` pair for cases where the two implementations legitimately diverge (wiki-only targets: publish_wiki.py links to the same-wiki page name, generate-docs-site.js links to the external wiki URL, since it isn't itself inside the wiki). `expectError: true` means this link must be reported as an unresolvable link by both implementations, with the original text left unchanged in the output — never a silent pass-through.

cases.name: fenced code block is never touched, even containing link-shaped text
image links are never touched (negative lookbehind on !)
external https URL is left untouched
anchor-only link (#fragment, no path) is left untouched
a [[bracketed]] literal with no .md and no / is left untouched (e.g. a TOML table name)
wikilink to a wiki-only target diverges: same-wiki link vs. external wiki URL
markdown link with custom display text to a wiki-only target preserves the custom text on both sides
link to a real file that isn't in the map at all resolves identically: a GitHub blob URL
link to a path that doesn't exist anywhere is an error on both sides, text left unchanged
relative path resolution from a subdirectory (../) lands on the correct target
a #fragment on a link to a mapped page is dropped, not carried through, on both sides
a site-only target (no "wiki" key in the map at all) diverges: publish_wiki.py falls back to a blob URL (wiki-target-blind by design), generate-docs-site.js resolves the real site route
